2603.28233.title: TwinMixing: A Shuffle-Aware Feature Interaction Model for Multi-Task Segmentation
2603.28233.paper_summary: Accurate and efficient perception is essential for autonomous driving, where segmentation tasks such as drivable-area and lane segmentation provide critical cues for motion planning and control. However, achieving high segmentation accuracy while maintaining real-time performance on low-cost hardware remains a challenging problem. To address this issue, we introduce TwinMixing, a lightweight multi-task segmentation model designed explicitly for drivable-area and lane segmentation. The proposed network features a shared encoder and task-specific decoders, enabling both feature sharing and task specialization. Within the encoder, we propose an Efficient Pyramid Mixing (EPM) module that enhances multi-scale feature extraction through a combination of grouped convolutions, depthwise dilated convolutions and channel shuffle operations, effectively expanding the receptive field while minimizing computational cost. Each decoder adopts a Dual-Branch Upsampling (DBU) Block composed of a learnable transposed convolution-based Fine detailed branch and a parameter-free bilinear interpolation-based Coarse grained branch, achieving detailed yet spatially consistent feature reconstruction. Extensive experiments on the BDD100K dataset validate the effectiveness of TwinMixing across three configurations - tiny, base, and large. Among them, the base configuration achieves the best trade-off between accuracy and computational efficiency, reaching 92.0% mIoU for drivable-area segmentation and 32.3% IoU for lane segmentation with only 0.43M parameters and 3.95 GFLOPs. Moreover, TwinMixing consistently outperforms existing segmentation models on the same tasks, as illustrated in Fig. 1. Thanks to its compact and modular design, TwinMixing demonstrates strong potential for real-time deployment in autonomous driving and embedded perception systems. The source code: https://github.com/Jun0se7en/TwinMixing.
2603.28233.paper_summary_zh: 準確且高效的感知對於自動駕駛至關重要，其中可駕駛區域和車道分割等分割任務為運動規劃和控制提供了關鍵線索。<lb/>然而，在低成本硬體上實現高分割精度同時保持實時性能仍然是一個挑戰性問題。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了TwinMixing，一種專門為可駕駛區域和車道分割設計的輕量級多任務分割模型。<lb/>所提出的網絡具有共享編碼器和任務特定解碼器，實現了特徵共享和任務專門化。<lb/>在編碼器內部，我們提出了一個高效金字塔混合（EPM）模塊，通過分組卷積、深度擴張卷積和通道洗牌操作的組合來增強多尺度特徵提取，有效擴大感受野，同時最小化計算成本。<lb/>每個解碼器採用一個雙分支上採樣（DBU）塊，由一個可學習的轉置卷積基礎的精細分支和一個無參數的雙線性插值基礎的粗糙分支組成，實現了細緻而空間一致的特徵重建。<lb/>在BDD100K數據集上的廣泛實驗驗證了TwinMixing在三種配置（tiny、base和large）下的有效性。<lb/>其中，base配置在準確性和計算效率之間達到了最佳平衡，對可駕駛區域分割達到92.0%的mIoU，對車道分割達到32.3%的IoU，僅需0.43M參數和3.95 GFLOPs。<lb/>此外，TwinMixing在相同任務上始終優於現有的分割模型，如圖1所示。<lb/>得益於其緊湊和模塊化的設計，TwinMixing顯示出在自動駕駛和嵌入式感知系統中實時部署的強大潛力。<lb/>源代碼：https://github.com/Jun0se7en/TwinMixing。
2603.28233.author: Minh-Khoi Do et.al.
2603.28233.authors: Minh-Khoi Do, Huy Che, Dinh-Duy Phan, Duc-Khai Lam, Duc-Lung Vu
